Today — your morning read in one glancei

Load the option chain, then click Refresh — this tab pulls the one key reading from every other tab into a single card.
The synthesis view. You built breadth across a dozen tabs; this pulls the single most important number from each into one glanceable morning card, ordered by how much it should drive decisions: the measured positioning first (Master Ladder, GEX regime, VWAP bias, key stats), then the calendar/ritual layer (Panchang, Rahu Kaal) last and clearly marked as context. If you read one tab before the open, read this one.

Legs

Δ-templatesi:
Stop Loss / Profit Lock (simplified)
Shows your P&L capped at these levels IF you exit exactly there — this does NOT simulate whether the underlying's actual path would trigger the exit before reaching a given terminal price (that needs path simulation, which this doesn't do). Treat the dotted line as "what realized P&L looks like with this exit discipline," not a forecast of when it fires.

Spot Resistance Wall Support Wall Max Pain
Everything on this tab — the regime read, composite signal, decision levels, and reversal signals — is my own original implementation built from transparent option-chain math (PCR, OI flow, max pain, OI walls, ATM straddle, put-call parity). It is not a reproduction of any third-party proprietary trading system — every input and threshold is visible above, nothing is a hidden formula. It's a rule-based read, not a prediction. Treat it as one input alongside your own analysis.
🛡 Portfolio Greeks — the risk deski
🌡 Stress heatmap — spot × IV shock
📓 Trade Journal — setup-tagged, stats per setupi
💼 Portfolio (open positions)i
📊 Performance Statisticsi

Open Trades

Closed Trades

Forward/paper trading only — opens with whatever live premiums the Builder shows when you click "Paper Trade This," tracks real day-by-day P&L going forward using live data. This is NOT a backtest (no historical data involved) and NOT a path simulation — just an honest log of what a position you actually opened would be worth right now, and what it was worth when you closed it.

Gann Analysisi

📊 Instrument: Using index spot i

Gann techniques are geometric/temporal heuristics, not statistical edges. These levels are deterministic transforms of the price you enter — treat them as one lens among many (confluence with OC levels, GEX, and VWAP is where they earn attention), never as standalone signals.

Market Levelsi

📊 Instrument: Using index spot i
Uses prev-day OHLC + session data + live spot. Data-measured panels need the updated server.

🧲 Max-Pain Magnet — measuredi
From your replay archive — click Load.
🕐 Time-of-Day Seasonalityi
From your replay archive — click Load.
Tiering, honestly: Camarilla / Murrey / round numbers carry real order-flow gravity in Indian markets because thousands of traders watch them — self-fulfilling, not mystical. Fib clusters are defensible via the confluence logic. Square-of-12 and harmonics sit in the same geometric tier as the Gann tab. The measured panels (TPO, max-pain, seasonality) are the strongest of all: they are YOUR recorded data answering "do these levels actually hold?" — trust them in that order.

Numerologyi

📊 Instrument: Using index spot i
Digit-Root Vibrationi
Day's Numerologyi
Vibration Levels — nearest strikes sharing the price's rooti
Digit-Root Wheel (1–9 cycle)i
Month's root-day calendari
Master Numbersi
Lo Shu Gridi
Special Numbers near pricei
Symbol Numerologyi
30-Day Root Wavei
🌙 Lunar Phases & Aspectsi
🕉 Panchang (Tithi / Nakshatra)i
Personal Day Cyclei
🔬 Digit Reality Check (measured)i
📈 Benford\'s Law (real number-law)i
Read this first. Numerology has no established predictive relationship with market prices — these are arithmetic patterns (digit sums, roots) computed from numbers you enter, presented because you asked for the tool, not because they carry a statistical edge. Unlike the OI, IV, and GEX panels elsewhere in this dashboard — which read real positioning data — nothing here is validated against outcomes. Treat it as a curiosity or a discipline-structuring ritual at most, never as a basis for risking capital. Position sizing and stops should always come from the data-driven tools.

Astrologyi

📊 Instrument: Using index spot i
Reads left→right: the sky ON [date] · IN [zodiac frame] · AGAINST [natal chart]. All three update live. Ephemeris: Sun ±0.01°, Moon ±0.3°, planets ±1°.
Zodiac Wheeli
Planetary Positionsi
Aspects in Effecti
Upcoming Exact Aspects (30d)i
Retrograde Stations (120d)i
Planetary Price Lines (all planets)i
📉 Bradley Siderograph — turn-date windowsi
🚪 Transit Ingress Calendar — sign-change dates + Pushya/Pradoshi
Nifty "Natal" Transitsi
☾ Moon Ingress & Void-of-Course (7d)i
Planetary Hours (today, Mumbai sunrise)i
📅 Calendar Seasonality (measured — the real "cycle")i
Day-of-week & turn-of-month return patterns from your replay archive — the documented anomalies planetary calendars orbit. Click Load.
Same honesty as the Numerology tab. The astronomy here is real — positions, aspects, retrogrades and the Vedic sidereal option are computed correctly from Keplerian elements, and you can check them against any ephemeris. The astrology — the idea that these configurations influence markets — has no established empirical support. Financial-astrology studies do not replicate. Use this as the calendar/context layer you enjoy, keep position sizing and stops with the data tools, and if a planetary line ever "works," remember the round-number and OI panels were probably at the same price.

Vedic Astrologyi

📊 Instrument: Using index spot i
Sidereal (Lahiri). Dasha needs birth time; without it, sub-periods drift.
🕉 Vimshottari Dasha — active period ladderi

Graha Drishti — Vedic aspects (whole-sign)i

Hora (planetary hour ruler)i

🪐 Stock lists by planetary ruler + commoditiesi
Navamsa (D9) positionsi
Ashtakavarga (transit strength)i
⭐ Confluence Filter — the Vedic "3-signal" rulei
Vedic method, honestly framed. This tab implements the genuinely Vedic tools the Western Astrology tab lacks — Vimshottari Dasha (the 120-year period system anchored to your birth Moon), the lunar nodes Rahu/Ketu, whole-sign Graha Drishti, and Choghadiya/Hora muhurta. The astronomy and the classical rules are computed correctly and you can check them against any panchang or Dasha calculator. But the market claim — that a planetary period or muhurta moves Nifty — has the same absence of replicated evidence as everything on the Astrology and Numerology tabs. Traditional Vedic financial astrology itself insists on confluence: at least three independent signals aligning before acting. The Confluence Filter panel makes that rule explicit — and it is still LORE, however many signals stack. Size from the data tabs; read this as heritage and timing ritual.

Validation — does the confluence actually work?i

🧪 Historical Astro Lab — the lore vs YEARS of datai
⚖️ Claims on Trial — the lore, scored against your own recorded daysi
Tests three testable claims from the esoteric tabs against the daily OHLC your replay archive has recorded: (1) narrow CPR → trend day, (2) full-moon days → weaker returns, (3) Bradley turns → price turns within ±4 days. History grows automatically every day the dashboard runs with the chain auto-refreshing.
🔬 Confluence Backtest — bounce rate by zone strengthi
The key question: do higher-confluence zones bounce more often? Runs against your replay archive. Click Run.
📋 Alert Outcome Journal — are your alerts signal or noise?i
Every fired alert, logged with what price did in the next ~20 min. Builds automatically as alerts fire. Click Load.
This is the tab that keeps the others honest. Everything else on the dashboard proposes levels; this tab tests them against what price actually did on your recorded days. The Confluence Backtest asks the one question that matters — do zones where more real positioning stacks up actually reverse price more often? — and the Alert Journal tells you whether the alerts firing in your ear are edge or noise. Two honest caveats: the backtest can only validate the data-backed confluence sources (OI, max-pain, round numbers) since the geometric/astrological levels aren\'t stored in replay; and small samples are noisy — treat everything here as firming up over weeks, not days. If a high-confluence bucket does NOT bounce more than a low one, believe the data, not the geometry.
This dashboard shows a rule-based read of current option-chain positioning (OI, change in OI, IV, PCR, max pain), plus a strategy library computed locally from live NSE premiums via your own nse_chain_server.py. Probability of Profit (POP) is a theoretical risk-neutral estimate from Black-Scholes using each strike's live implied volatility — it assumes lognormal returns and constant IV, not a guarantee or backtested win rate. Max profit/loss and breakevens for same-expiry strategies are exact given current premiums and will shift as premiums move; calendar/diagonal spread economics assume the far leg's IV stays constant until near-leg expiry. Margin for defined-risk spreads is approximated as max loss (the standard post-2021 SEBI spread-margin benefit); margin for undefined-risk strategies (short straddle/strangle, ratio backspreads) is a rough estimate only — always confirm with your broker's margin calculator before trading. This is informational only, not investment advice or a trade recommendation.
🗂 Panel Manager
Hidden panels skip rendering too — lighter refreshes.
